threat_model: Personal single-user Android app, no server/accounts. Assets: Gemini API key (Keystore-wrapped AES/GCM in DataStore), personal mistake data in Room (unencrypted, accepted), screenshots/thumbnails. Attack surfaces: exported MainActivity share intent, Gemini REST client, WorkManager.

verified_ok.detail: LlmClient.kt sends key in x-goog-api-key header, never in URL; baseUrl hardcoded https; targetSdk 35 default blocks cleartext; no usesCleartextTraffic or networkSecurityConfig override in manifest.

No Log call touches the key; only 4 Log.w in ImportViewModel about URIs/dimensions. LlmResult reason strings carry HTTP codes and IOException messages only. SettingsScreen test-connection passes the key per-call; status strings never include it.
ApiKeyStore: AES/GCM key generated inside AndroidKeyStore (non-exportable), IV:ciphertext base64 in DataStore; PasswordVisualTransformation on input; clear() removes ciphertext.
allowBackup=false in manifest; on API 31+ this also opts out of D2D transfer, so absent dataExtractionRules is acceptable.
All Room queries parameterized; no rawQuery or string concatenation anywhere.
GeminiClient.parse treats responses as untrusted: kotlinx-serialization with ignoreUnknownKeys, catch-all to Failed, text rendered only via Compose Text; no WebView/fromHtml in the repo.
inSampleSize = max(1, maxDim/2000) with maxDim capped at 8000: decoded bitmap at most ~4000px transiently, 2000px typical; no overflow.
No AIza-pattern keys; tests use a fake test-key only; .gitignore excludes keystores, secrets.properties, personal screenshot samples; gradle wrapper URL is https.
Only exported component is MainActivity (launcher + SEND filters); no deep links; SEND extras parsed type-safely on API 33+.

findings.title: Share-intent handler buffers the entire untrusted stream into memory before any size check (OOM DoS)
findings.detail: loadUnsafe() reads the full stream with readBytes() before the MAX_DIMENSION_PX bounds check at line 196. Both the URI and the image/* MIME type come from the sending app and are never validated against content. A malicious or buggy app (or an accidental user share) can hand a multi-GB stream; readBytes() buffers it entirely into a ByteArray and the process dies with OutOfMemoryError before the dimension rejection runs. The dimension cap defeats decode bombs but not raw-stream bombs. Impact is crash-only on a single-user device (per-screenshot saves survive), hence P3, but this is the one reachable attack through the app's only exported surface.
findings.attack_path: malicious app -> ACTION_SEND image/* with huge content:// or file:// stream -> MainActivity.handleShareIntent -> pendingShares -> ImportViewModel.loadUnsafe readBytes() -> OOM crash
findings.fix: Stream-copy into a bounded buffer and reject anything over a generous ceiling (e.g. 50 MB, far above any real screenshot), discarding with a Log.w like the other rejection branches; or decode bounds from a first openInputStream and only then read bytes.

findings.title: Keystore decrypt failure silently swallowed: stale ciphertext persists and the generation worker retries forever behind a misleading No-key-saved UI
findings.detail: load() catches every exception and returns null. Two consequences: (1) the undecryptable ciphertext blob stays in DataStore indefinitely since clear() never runs on this path, leaving a dead secret blob in app storage; (2) MnemonicRepository.generatePending() returns false when load() is null, so GenerationWorker returns Result.retry and WorkManager retries indefinitely with no user-visible failure, while SettingsScreen reports No key saved. Silent key loss on keystore reset is an acceptable availability trade per the plan, but the stale-blob plus infinite-retry combination should be a deliberate decision, not an accident of the catch-all.
findings.fix: On decrypt failure in load(), remove the pref entry (self-heal) so downstream state is consistent, and optionally surface a stored-key-unrecoverable message in Settings.

findings.title: Personal screenshot thumbnails written to filesDir/thumbs but never read and never deleted (unbounded retention of recitation data)
findings.detail: saveThumbnail() persists a JPEG of every confirmed screenshot under filesDir/thumbs/<sha256>.jpg and stores the path in ImportScreenshotEntity.thumbnailPath. Grep confirms thumbnailPath is written but never read by any screen, and no code deletes thumbnail files or ImportScreenshotEntity rows: MistakeRepository.delete() removes spot, occurrences, and review log but leaves the import row and thumbnail on disk forever. Files are app-private with allowBackup=false, so this is retention hygiene rather than an exploitable leak, but data the user believes permanently removed survives with no purge path. Hash-based filenames (SHA-256 of original bytes) make collision or overwrite a non-issue.
findings.fix: Either wire thumbnails into the archive UI with a deletion path, or stop writing them until the consuming feature exists; add thumbnail cleanup when the referencing import data is deleted.

suppressed.candidate: Arbitrary-readable-URI ingestion: file:// shares make the app read any file it can access
suppressed.reason: Content is only hashed and bitmap-decoded; non-images fail decode and are discarded; nothing is exfiltrated, uploaded, or rendered unless it is a valid image the sender could already read. No confidentiality impact in this architecture.

suppressed.candidate: IOException message surfaced in Settings status / Retryable reason
suppressed.reason: May contain hostname at most, never key material; shown only to the device owner.
Verified: MnemonicRepository.buildPrompt uses only canonical dataset verse text and references; no personal-data path into the prompt.

residual_risks: scripts/fetch-datasets.sh downloads dataset JSON from two third-party GitHub repos over HTTPS without commit pinning or checksum verification; a compromised upstream could ship altered Quran text or malformed JSON into the app bundle (worst case in-app: wrong text or a parse crash; no code-execution path via kotlinx JSON).
Room DB (mistake history) is unencrypted at rest; explicitly accepted in the plan for the single-user threat model.
Release build has isMinifyEnabled=false; no security impact for a BYO-key app (nothing secret ships in the APK).
testing_gaps: No test covers share-intent ingestion with hostile inputs (oversized stream, non-image bytes with image/* MIME, zero-byte stream); the SEC-1 byte cap should land with a test.
No test exercises ApiKeyStore failure modes (corrupt blob, missing keystore alias); the interface is faked in JVM tests so the catch-all in load() never executes in CI.
No test asserts thumbnail cleanup; one would have caught the SEC-3 dead write.
